today_summary: GCM26 settled $4,076.50 Wednesday (07/22), up $84.50 off Tuesday's $3,992.20 swing low. ATM IV sits at 19.91% (7 DTE), the lowest reading of the week. Chain stays call-heavy (P/C OI 0.54, 137,946 call OI vs 74,298 put OI) with the nearest OI walls at $4,000 (put, 4,116 contracts) and $4,700 (call, 4,912 contracts). Max pain $4,795 sits $718.50 above spot, still exerting a long-range upward pull into expiry.
five_day_thesis: Over the last five sessions spot round-tripped from $4,051.90 down to a $3,992.20 low on 07/17 and back up to $4,076.50 — a net +$24.60 gain that masks a roughly $130 intraweek drawdown and recovery. Max pain drifted higher throughout, $4,750 → $4,790 → $4,795 → $4,800 → $4,795 (+$45 net), while the structural walls didn't move at all: put wall pinned at $4,000 and call wall at $5,370 for all five sessions, meaning the OI skeleton held even as price swung 2%. The real story is under the hood — near-spot net GEX (±20% window) went from firmly negative (-96.8 on 07/17, -59.7 on 07/21) to essentially flat at -0.79 today, the least short-gamma reading of the cycle, while ATM IV compressed 21.6% → 19.91% (-1.69pp). The gamma flip strike crept up from $3,195 to $3,280 (+$85) in step with spot's bounce, but at nearly $800 below current price dealers remain short gamma across the entire range price actually trades — the flattening is a tailwind for continued upside, not yet a regime change.
what_changed: Spot recovered $84.50 off Tuesday's $3,992.20 low to close $4,076.50, still $718.50 below max pain
Near-spot net GEX (±20% window) flattened from -59.7 to -0.79 day-over-day — least short-gamma reading of the past five sessions
ATM IV compressed 20.47% → 19.91% (7 DTE), extending a five-day slide from 21.6%
Max pain oscillated in a tight $10 band all week ($4,790–$4,800), settling at $4,795
Put wall held at $4,000 (4,116 contracts) and call wall at $5,370 (8,008 contracts) unchanged for a fifth straight session
Gamma flip strike rose $3,225 → $3,280, tracking spot's bounce but staying roughly $800 under current price
prediction: Bias is cautiously bullish while spot holds above the $4,000 put wall / $3,992 swing low. With max pain $718 above spot and IV compressing into a flattening GEX backdrop, the path of least resistance is a grind toward the $4,150–$4,200 near-term call-OI cluster. A close back below $4,000 reactivates the deeply negative-gamma regime under the $3,280 flip strike and risks acceleration toward $3,900.

day_trade_cta.bias_note: Five-day recovery off the $3,992 low plus GEX flattening to -0.79 favor continued upside; flips bearish on a close below $4,000.
day_trade_cta.entry_note: Buy dips into the $4,050 call-OI shelf (13,960 contracts, local positive GEX pocket) with volume confirmation; avoid chasing extension above $4,100.
day_trade_cta.pivot_note: Sustained trade above opens the path to $4,150 then $4,200. Rejection here keeps price capped inside the week's IV-compression range.
day_trade_cta.stop_note: -2.1% from entry midpoint, just under the week's $3,992 swing low. A close under the $4,000 put wall reactivates the short-gamma cascade toward $3,900.
day_trade_cta.target_note: T1 $4,150 is the strongest near-spot positive-GEX strike (net GEX +6.1) — take 50% off. T2 $4,200 is the next call cluster (net GEX +13.4) — exit remainder.
day_trade_cta.risk_note: 1-sigma daily move ≈ $51 on 19.91% IV. Tight-window GEX (±$200 of spot) is still net negative at -83.3 despite the wider window flattening — expect amplified, whippy moves; size 20-30% below normal.
